channel.title: Teaching Resources on MathTR
channel.description: Recent content in Teaching Resources on MathTR

item.description: <!DOCTYPE html> Grade Calculator Overall Grade Calculator How to use the grade calculator Enter weights of assessments. Make sure that the weights you enter are in the same scale. Enter your scores accordingly. Leave the boxes blank if you don't have that type of assessment. Type of Assessment Weight Score(in 100-point scale) Homework Average Test Average Final Exam Quiz Average Attendance/In-class Performance Lab Assignment Project Please calculate my grade. Based on what you entered, your overall grade is /100.

<!DOCTYPE html> MA119 Learning Goals MA119 Learning Goals There are in total learning goals. Check the goals you have mastered and click the following button to see your level of mastery. Check My Level of Mastery Congratulation! You have mastered about of learning goals. Algebra Preliminaries R-1. Arithmetic I can simplify fractions. I can perform the operations addition, subtraction, multiplication and division on real numbers using order of operations. I can evaluate and simplify arithmetic expressions without the use of a calculator.

<!DOCTYPE html> MA441 Learning Goals MA441 Learning Goals There are in total learning goals. Check the goals you have mastered and click the following button to see your level of mastery. Check My Level of Mastery Congratulation! You have mastered about of learning goals. Algebra and Geometry of Functions F1: I can use function notations to describe functions. F2: I can evaluate and graph functions. F3: I can find domain, range and intercepts of a function.

item.title: Course Slides
item.description: Slides for MA336 Topic 1: Statistical Studies Topic 2: Summarizing Data Graphically Topic 3: Summarizing Data Numerically Topic 4: Linear Relationship Topic 5: Introduction to Probability Topic 6: Two-way Tables Topic 7: Discrete Random Variables Topic 8: Continuous Random Variables Topic 9: Sampling Distribution Topic 10: Confidence Interval for a Mean Topic 11: Confidence Interval for a Proportion Topic 12: Concepts of Hypothesis Testing Topic 13: Hypothesis Testing for a Mean or a Proportion Topic 14: Confidence Interval for a Median Slides for MA321 Lesson 1: Fundamentals of Statistics Lesson 2: Statistical Study - Trust or Not Lesson 3: Statistical Tables and Graph Lesson 4: Graphics in the Media Lesson 5: Correlation and Causality Lesson 6: Characterizing Data Lesson 7: Measures of Variation Lesson 8: The Normal Distribution Lesson 9: Statistical Inference Lesson 10: Understand, Solve, and Explain Lesson 11: Problem-Solving with Units Lesson 12: Problem-Solving Guidelines and Hints Lesson 13: Mathematics and Music Lesson 14: Perspective and Symmetry Lesson 15: Proportion and the Golden Ratio Lesson 16: Functions - The Building Blocks of Mathematical Models Lesson 17: Linear Modeling

item.title: Excel Labs for MA321
item.description: List of lab instructions Lab 1: Introduction Lab 2: Frequency Tables Lab 3: Scatterplot Lab 4: Descriptive Statistics Lab 5: Normal Distribution Lab 6: Unit Conversion Lab 7: Exponentiation Lab 8: Functions

item.title: Lab instructions
item.description: Lab instruction of MA321 Lab 1: Introduction Lab 2: Frequency Tables Lab 3: Scatterplot Lab 4: Descriptive Statistics Lab 5: Normal Distribution Lab 6: Unit Conversion Lab 7: Exponetiation Lab 8: Functions

item.title: OERs

item.title: Resources for Teachers
item.description: Presentations on Tools for Teachers How to Self-Publish a Book Online Using Bookdown Rmarkdown and Other Productivity Tools for Math Teachers Exam Policies with Timer Exam Policies with Real Time Clock and Timer

item.title: Slides for MA321 Mathematics in Contemporary Society
item.description: List of course slides Lesson 1: Fundamentals of Statistics Lesson 2: Statistical Study - Trust or Not Lesson 3: Statistical Tables and Graph Lesson 4: Graphics in the Media Lesson 5: Correlation and Causality Lesson 6: Characterizing Data Lesson 7: Measures of Variation Lesson 8: The Normal Distribution Lesson 9: Statistical Inference Lesson 10: Understand, Solve, and Explain Lesson 11: Problem-Solving with Units Lesson 12: Problem-Solving Guidelines and Hints Lesson 13: Mathematics and Music Lesson 14: Perspective and Symmetry Lesson 15: Proportion and the Golden Ratio Lesson 16: Functions - The Building Blocks of Mathematical Models Lesson 17: Linear Modeling
